     The Numbers Business Report

Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $54,513,740Details
International Box Office $106,644,611
Worldwide Box Office $161,158,351
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,486,437
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,534,015
Total $6,020,452
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After months have passed without a culprit in her daughter’s murder case, Mildred Hayes makes a bold move, painting three signs leading into her town with a controversial message directed at William Willoughby, the town’s revered chief of police. When his second-in-command Officer Dixon, an immature mother’s boy with a penchant for violence, gets involved, the battle between Mildred and Ebbing’s law enforcement is only exacerbated.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$322,168 (0.6% of total gross)
Legs:12.38 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:33.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$12,000,000 (worldwide box office is 13.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/1,726 max. theaters, 8.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $72,063,126

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 10th, 2017 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
December 1st, 2017 (Expands Wide) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: December 29th, 2017 (Wide) (Australia)
January 5th, 2018 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 12th, 2018 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
January 12th, 2018 (Wide), released as Tres anuncios en las afueras (Spain)
January 12th, 2018 (Wide), released as Tre manifesti a Ebbing, Missouri (Italy)
Video Release: February 13th, 2018 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for violence, language throughout, and some sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2468 (Cert #50861), 3/22/2017)
Running Time: 115 minutes
Award nomination: 2018 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Film Winner, Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Screen Actors Guild Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture Winner
Plot point: Bigotry, Cancer, Corrupt Cops, Death of a Sibling, Death of a Son or Daughter, Dysfunctional Family, Murder Mystery, Non-Chronological, Police Brutality, Police Procedural, Sex Crimes, Suicide, Terminal Illness
Subgenre: Black Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Black Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Fox Searchlight Pictures, Film4, Blueprint Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

After months have passed without a culprit in her daughter’s murder case, Mildred Hayes makes a bold move, painting three signs leading into her town with a controversial message directed at William Willoughby, the town’s revered chief of police. When his second-in-command Officer Dixon, an immature mother’s boy with a penchant for violence, gets involved, the battle between Mildred and Ebbing’s law enforcement is only exacerbated.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$322,168 (0.6% of total gross)
Legs:12.38 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:33.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$12,000,000 (worldwide box office is 13.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/1,726 max. theaters, 8.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $72,063,126

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 10th, 2017 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
December 1st, 2017 (Expands Wide) by Fox Searchlight

MPA Rating: R for violence, language throughout, and some sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2468 (Cert #50861), 3/22/2017)
Running Time: 115 minutes
Award nomination: 2018 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Film Winner, Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Screen Actors Guild Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture Winner
